macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US ADP Employment Change Expected at 109K for Latest Report
items.summary: The upcoming US ADP Employment Change is forecasted at 109K, compared to prior 61K consensus and 99K actual, scheduled for 12:30 PM amid key economic data releases. This private payrolls figure often previews nonfarm payrolls and influences Fed rate expectations.

items.title: Euro Area PPI YoY Comes in at 2.1%
items.summary: Euro Area Producer Price Index year-over-year rose 2.1%, beating forecasts of -3% and previous 1.8%, at 09:00 AM. This data impacts ECB inflation outlook and monetary policy decisions.

items.title: Meta and Microsoft Stocks Decline After Disappointing Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Meta and Microsoft fell following their latest earnings reports, as discussed by Barron's Roundtable panelists. Investors are advised to focus on key market considerations amid the downturn.

items.title: Broad Market Rally Driven by Tech Earnings and Gold Surge
items.summary: Markets rallied broadly with investors turning to gold and metals amid Middle East peace hopes and robust tech earnings, according to Hennion & Walsh CIO Kevin Mahn. Northwestern Mutual CIO attributes gains to resilient growth and stable labor market.

items.title: Apple Highlights AI Investments in Q2 Earnings Report
items.summary: Apple's second-quarter earnings featured CEO Tim Cook emphasizing major investments in artificial intelligence. The report contributed to market discussions on Big Tech performance.

items.title: Upcoming CPI Data Expected to Show 0.6% MoM and 3.7% YoY Rise
items.summary: Headline CPI for April is forecasted to increase 0.6% month-over-month and 3.7% year-over-year, with core CPI at 0.3% MoM and 2.7% YoY. This week's light calendar also features April nonfarm payrolls.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Sees $179M in Liquidations Over 24 Hours, Shorts Hit Hardest
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced $179 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, with short positions accounting for $129 million and long positions $50.15 million; BTC and ETH saw $12.68M and $18.52M liquidated respectively, affecting 77,665 traders.

items.title: Crypto Market Liquidations Reach $202M in 24 Hours
items.summary: Total cryptocurrency liquidations hit $202 million over the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin shorts liquidated for $19.48 million and longs for $8.44 million, alongside significant Ethereum position liquidations.

items.title: Bitcoin Briefly Slips Below $80,000 But Options Traders Bet on Quick Recovery
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped below $80,000 in the last 24 hours amid profit-taking and over-leveraged position unwinding, but recovered to $80,360 as options markets show strong risk appetite and no signs of deeper breakdown.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $80,000 After Rejection at $82,500 Amid ETF Outflows
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near $80,000 following a rejection at $82,500, with cautious sentiment triggered by ETF outflows on Thursday.

items.title: Coinbase Reports $394M Q1 Loss and Major Outage
items.summary: Coinbase posted a $394 million Q1 loss due to $482 million in unrealized crypto investment losses tied to Bitcoin's price slide, followed by an hours-long outage from an AWS data center overheating.

items.title: Hut 8 Revenue Triples to $71M, Signs $9.8B AI Lease
items.summary: Bitcoin miner Hut 8 reported Q1 revenue of $71 million, up threefold year-over-year, and inked a 15-year $9.8 billion lease to provide AI computing power from a Texas facility.

items.title: SoFi to Launch SoFiUSD on Solana Network
items.summary: Regulated US bank SoFi announced expansion of SoFiUSD to Solana, becoming the first nationally chartered US bank to accept direct Solana network deposits.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Mythos, Achieving 77.8% on SWE-bench Pro and 14.5-Hour Autonomous Task Horizon
items.summary: Anthropic announced Claude Mythos, scoring nearly 20 points higher than competitors on SWE-bench Pro and demonstrating a 50% task horizon of 14 hours and 30 minutes on meter evaluation, far surpassing other models in unsupervised task completion.

items.title: Google Chrome Introduces Experimental Prompt API for AI Models like Gemini in JavaScript
items.summary: Chrome's new Prompt API enables direct use of AI models such as Gemini from JavaScript, with Mozilla raising concerns about web neutrality and interoperability; Google and Microsoft are exploring interoperability solutions.

items.title: ServiceNow Announces Autonomous Data Governance, Analytics, and CRM at Knowledge Event
items.summary: ServiceNow unveiled autonomous data governance capabilities, conversational AI-driven data analytics, and autonomous CRM providing a unified customer view with pre-triaged support cases, emphasizing agentic AI integration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced with PS5 Demo Available Now
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo revealed Nioh 3 during June's PlayStation State of Play, allowing soulslike fans to play a demo immediately on PS5.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft unveiled the full list of games joining Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.

items.title: Bloodstained Sequel 'The Scarlet Engagement' Revealed at State of Play
items.summary: Developer ArtPlay announced the next Bloodstained game, looking vastly different from Ritual of the Night, during June's PlayStation State of Play.

items.title: Sony Records $560 Million Loss on Bungie Acquisition Amid Struggles
items.summary: Sony reports a massive new loss on its Bungie purchase as Marathon faces issues and Destiny 2 player numbers decline sharply.

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles Set for September Launch
items.summary: A remaster of Final Fantasy Tactics titled The Ivalice Chronicles was announced for release in September during June's State of Play.

items.title: Silent Hill f Release Date Revealed, Coming Sooner Than Expected
items.summary: The upcoming Silent Hill f received a release date at June's PlayStation State of Play, arriving earlier than anticipated.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Announces ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 revealed a new Force of Law Warbond, giving players fresh content in the cooperative shooter.

items.title: Original Four Mortal Kombat Games Coming to PS4 and PS5
items.summary: Players will soon access the first four Mortal Kombat titles on PlayStation 4 and 5.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Signs Preliminary Deal to Produce Chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly signed a preliminary agreement to manufacture chips for Apple, echoing their past partnership and potentially hedging Apple's chip strategy.

items.title: Nintendo Raises Switch 2 Prices Amid Chip Crisis
items.summary: Nintendo is increasing prices for the Switch 2 due to ongoing chip shortages, despite selling 19.86 million units last year, and forecasts a sales drop next year.

items.title: Coinbase Outage Blamed on AWS Datacenter Overheating
items.summary: Crypto trading platform Coinbase suffered a major outage due to multiple chiller failures causing overheating in an AWS US-EAST-1 datacenter, resolved after impacting services.

items.title: Instructure Hackers Claim Data Theft from 9,000 Schools
items.summary: Hackers attacking Instructure claim to have stolen data from nearly 9,000 schools, locked students out of the Canvas platform, and demand negotiation by May 12.

items.title: Discord Suffers Outage from API System Issues
items.summary: Discord experienced a widespread outage affecting some users, caused by problems with its API systems, but services have since been restored.

items.title: Sony Partners with TSMC for Image Sensor Production
items.summary: Sony is collaborating with TSMC to build a new facility for advanced image sensor manufacturing using the chip giant's expertise.

items.title: Instagram Removes End-to-End Encryption from Direct Messages
items.summary: Meta has discontinued end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs, allowing access to chat content and raising privacy concerns for users.

items.title: Microsoft's New CPU Trick Fixes Windows 11 App Stutters
items.summary: Microsoft introduces a new CPU optimization feature and Low Latency Profile in Windows 11, along with the K2 project, to eliminate app stuttering issues.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Can Predict Fainting Up to 5 Minutes in Advance
items.summary: Samsung claims its Galaxy Watch can detect signs of fainting up to five minutes ahead, enhancing health monitoring capabilities.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to scale the mobile OS to desktops for unified computing.
